key_departures.impact: Rotational pass rusher who was one of the few edge contributors on the roster. Departed to division rival Colts. Loss is partially offset by the Jermaine Johnson trade acquisition.
Was traded by Tennessee mid-season to Baltimore, then became a FA and signed with New England (3yr/$39M). Originally a Titan, his exit further depleted the pass-rush group prior to free agency.

Traded to Jets in exchange for EDGE Jermaine Johnson (Feb. 26). Sweat was PFF's 5th-ranked interior DL and a near-All-Pro run-stopper on a cheap rookie deal. His departure creates an IDL depth hole and shifts the interior profile significantly under Saleh's 4-3 scheme.

Expected to be released imminently. Signed to 4yr/$76.4M deal with $51.5M guaranteed in 2024, but played only 12 games across two injury-plagued seasons in Tennessee. Pending release saves ~$12M in cap space with ~$8.1M dead money.

Pending release/restructure. Failed $23M/yr Carthon-era signing that produced just 4 TDs in two seasons. Suffered broken fibula in November 2025, appeared in only 7 games. Robinson signing makes him largely redundant. Cut decision expected before March 16 guarantee trigger.
Released in late February. Was the starting center. Creates an immediate hole at the position that Austin Schlottman is now being brought in to fill.
Released in late February. Was a depth/rotational safety. Minimal starter-level impact but thins the safety depth.

signings.analysis: Acquired via player-for-player trade (T'Vondre Sweat to Jets) on Feb. 26. Johnson is on his fifth-year option ($13.41M fully guaranteed) and reunites with Robert Saleh who coached him with the Jets. He had a Pro Bowl 2023 (7.5 sacks) but suffered a torn Achilles in 2024 and was limited to 3 sacks in 14 games in 2025. One-year deal only — this is a buy-low reclamation project, not a long-term solution. EDGE need remains real. Partially addressed for 2026 but Tennessee will want to address this position in the draft for the future.

Tennessee's top free agency target. 3yr/$63M with $42M guaranteed — elite-tier money for an interior rusher who had 14.5 sacks over two seasons in Denver (7.5 in 2025). Reunites with Saleh and DL coach Aaron Whitecotton from their Jets days together. At 6-4, 288 lbs, can play DT or 5-tech and is a legitimate interior pass rusher. Two years of effective guarantees make him the real deal. Pairs with Jeffery Simmons to give Tennessee a dominant interior tandem. IDL drops from a critical need to solidly addressed. Also offsets the loss of T'Vondre Sweat as a pass-rushing presence, though the run-stopping profile is different.

4yr/$70M ($39.98M fully guaranteed per SI's Albert Breer) — some reports put total value at $78M but the $70M/$39.98M guaranteed figure is the most precisely sourced. Robinson had a career-high 1,014 yards and 4 TDs in 2025 playing for Daboll with the Giants and recorded 185 catches over his last two seasons. Instantly becomes WR1 for Cam Ward. The guarantee structure (just under $40M fully gtd) reflects real commitment — roughly two years of guaranteed money. However, Robinson is a slot-heavy receiver (6-foot), not a true big-bodied outside WR1. Tennessee still lacks a true downfield X receiver. WR need is partially addressed — the slot/possession role is filled at starter level, but a complementary outside WR remains a legitimate draft need.

3yr/$60M with $42M guaranteed — legitimate starter-level investment. Taylor allowed only 1.0 yards per coverage snap in 2025 playing primarily in the slot for New Orleans. Has inside-outside versatility and brings a physical, blitzing element that fits Saleh's aggressive defensive scheme. The $42M guaranteed signals real long-term commitment. Paired with Flott, the Titans have addressed their most critical defensive need with two multi-year contracts. CB1 role is solidly filled. Despite some concerns about whether his 2025 performance was sustainable, the Titans are committed.

3yr/$45M with $32M fully guaranteed per Mike Garafolo — solid CB2 investment. Flott started 14 games for the Giants in 2025, posting a career-high 11 pass deflections and 1 INT. At 6-2, gives the Titans the size at the position they have been seeking. Will line up as an outside CB in Saleh's scheme. Combined with Taylor (also 3yr deal), the Titans have comprehensively rebuilt their CB room with two starting-caliber players on multi-year commitments. CB need is fully addressed for the near term.

2yr/$10M — depth signing to round out a completely overhauled CB room. Williams played sparingly in coverage for the Chiefs (15% of defensive snaps) but was a significant special teams contributor (255 ST snaps, 3rd on team). Two-year deal at depth money. Per ESPN, 'Williams will have to compete for a roster spot' despite the two-year term. Provides CB depth and special teams value but does not change the need picture — Taylor and Flott are the starters.

3yr/$42M (per Music City Miracles; PFF tracker lists 3yr/$24M — guarantee details not yet reported, listed as TBD). Reunites with Daboll who drafted him in the 4th round in 2022. At 6-6, 255 lbs, Bellinger is primarily an in-line blocking TE who will complement existing starter Gunnar Helm in 12-personnel packages. He posted only 19 catches in 2025 and is not a prominent receiving threat. The Titans were also potentially losing Chig Okonkwo to free agency. Bellinger adds a quality blocking presence and Daboll familiarity but does not fully solve the receiving TE role — Helm remains the primary pass-catching option. TE need partially addressed. NOTE: Contract total value conflicting between sources ($24M per PFF/Arrowhead Pride vs. $42M per Music City Miracles); using $42M figure from the most Titans-specific sourcing, but marking confidence as medium.

2-year deal (financial terms not yet reported). Reunites with OC Brian Daboll who worked with Trubisky in Buffalo in 2021. Will back up Cam Ward and bring familiarity with Daboll's offensive system — a meaningful upgrade over Brandon Allen who served in this role in 2025. Trubisky completed 70%+ of passes as a backup in 2024-25 with 6 TDs and 0 INTs per Music City Miracles. This signals the likely end of Will Levis's tenure in Tennessee. Trubisky is a veteran mentor/backup — QB1 need (Cam Ward) was never a need, and the backup role is now addressed adequately. NOTE: Contract financial terms not yet publicly confirmed.

1-year deal (terms not yet reported per Tom Pelissero). Schlottman played in Daboll's system with the Giants — made 4 starts in 2025 but was primarily a backup. Expected to compete for and potentially win the starting center job vacated by the released Lloyd Cushenberry III. One-year deal is a bridge, not a solution. Tennessee tried to land Tyler Linderbaum but lost him to the Raiders. IOL remains a meaningful need — Schlottman is a depth/bridge starter, not a long-term answer. Team will likely address center in the draft. NOTE: Contract financial terms not yet publicly confirmed.

1yr/up to $4.215M (per Ian Rapoport; Yahoo Sports reports $2.5M base). Volson started 15 games at RG for the Bengals in 2024 but missed all of 2025 due to right shoulder surgery. When healthy, is a viable starting guard option. One-year prove-it deal at depth money. Provides guard depth/competition but the injury history and short-term structure keep this from moving the IOL needle significantly. IOL need remains — the Titans are working with bridge solutions at center and are relying on incumbents (Peter Skoronski) and depth pieces at guard.

2yr/$8M — depth interior DL signing to round out the rotation alongside Simmons and Franklin-Myers. Elliott provides run-stopping depth and rotational pass rush at well-below-market money. Does not change the need picture — Franklin-Myers already fully addressed the IDL starter need. This is roster filler.
2yr/$6M. Former Chiefs and Texans punter upgrades the special teams unit. Not a positional need in the draft context but a solid special teams acquisition.
Re-signed the five-time Pro Bowl long snapper who has been with the Titans since 2021. One-year deal. Special teams continuity move only — no draft need implications.

recommended_need_adjustments.reasoning: Tennessee signed THREE cornerbacks on Day 1 of free agency, headlined by Alontae Taylor (3yr/$60M, $42M gtd) and Cor'Dale Flott (3yr/$45M, $32M gtd). Both are multi-year, starter-level investments with significant guarantees. With the pending release of L'Jarius Sneed, the room is being rebuilt from scratch with quality players. CB drops to a luxury/developmental pick only. May still draft a CB Day 2-3 for depth but it is no longer a critical need.

John Franklin-Myers signing (3yr/$63M, $42M gtd) fills the void created by the T'Vondre Sweat trade. Franklin-Myers had 14.5 sacks over two seasons and pairs with All-Pro Jeffery Simmons to form an elite interior tandem. Two years of effective guarantees indicate real commitment. Jordan Elliott provides additional rotation depth (2yr/$8M). IDL goes from critical need to addressed — may still want rotational depth in middle rounds of the draft but this is no longer a top-3 need. Slight downgrade from tier 1 to tier 3 (not tier 4) because Sweat's run-stuffing profile is not fully replicated and Franklin-Myers is entering his age-30 season.

Wan'Dale Robinson signing (4yr/$70M, $39.98M gtd) provides a legitimate WR1 target for Cam Ward in Daboll's system. Robinson had 1,014 yards and was highly productive in this exact scheme. However, Robinson is a slot-heavy, possession receiver — not a true downfield outside WR1. The pending release of Calvin Ridley means the team still lacks a complementary outside threat opposite Robinson. WR drops from a tier 1 (most critical) to a tier 2 need — they have their slot/WR1 presence, but a bigger outside receiver to pair with Robinson remains a real draft priority. If Ridley is retained, this may stay at tier 2; if cut, it arguably stays there or edges up.

The Jermaine Johnson trade provides a starter-level EDGE presence for 2026, but it's a one-year, $13.41M deal on his fifth-year option, coming off a torn Achilles and a disappointing 2025 (3 sacks). Johnson is a buy-low reclamation — if healthy, he's valuable; if not, the depth is thin (only Jaylen Harrell and Oluwafemi Oladejo under contract as backups). Arden Key departed to Indy and Dre'Mont Jones left for New England. Franklin-Myers adds interior pass rush but not traditional edge rush. EDGE remains a significant priority in the draft — drops from tier 1 critical to tier 2 because there's a starting-caliber player in place for 2026, but the long-term solution and true depth still need to be found via the draft.

The Titans tried to land Tyler Linderbaum at center but lost him to the Raiders. They instead signed Austin Schlottman (1yr, bridge starter) and Cordell Volson (1yr/~$2.5-4.2M, injury reclamation at guard). Lloyd Cushenberry III was released. Both IOL signings are short-term bridge solutions — one-year deals with no long-term commitment. Peter Skoronski remains at LG as a building block. IOL need is unchanged at tier 2 — center in particular remains a priority for the draft, and the overall interior line depth is thin. The team essentially kicked the can down the road at this position and will need a Day 1 or Day 2 answer in April.

Daniel Bellinger signing (3yr/$42M or $24M depending on source — see note) adds a starting-caliber in-line blocker alongside Gunnar Helm. However, Bellinger is primarily a run-blocker (19 catches in 2025), not a true receiving threat. With Chig Okonkwo potentially departing in free agency, the receiving TE role is still not fully addressed. Helm is the primary pass-catcher. TE remains a tier 3 need — not critical, but a receiving TE via the draft (Rounds 3-5) would still be additive and is likely on the board.

Xavier Woods was released (late February) but was a rotational piece. Amani Hooker and Quandre Diggs remain. No safety was added in free agency. Safety depth is thinner post-Woods release but the starters are intact. Tier 3 need — depth addition in the middle rounds of the draft is reasonable but not urgent.

No offensive tackle additions in free agency. JC Latham (RT) and Dan Moore Jr. (LT) remain as the starting bookends. No departures at OT. Need unchanged — a developmental or competition piece via the draft is possible but not a top priority.

key_additions.headline: Elite interior pass rusher (14.5 sacks over 2 seasons) reunites with Saleh to pair with Jeffery Simmons in an upgraded front four
Top CB market investment anchors a completely rebuilt Titans secondary with inside-outside versatility
1,000-yard receiver reunites with OC Daboll to become Cam Ward's primary target in Year 2
6-2 outside CB starter signed to multi-year deal completes the overhaul of Tennessee's desperate cornerback room

key_additions.previous_team: New York Jets (trade for T'Vondre Sweat)
key_additions.headline: Former Pro Bowler reunites with Saleh on a prove-it year after torn Achilles — buy-low bet on a key pass-rush reclamation
Daboll-drafted in-line TE adds blocking presence to complement Gunnar Helm in the Titans' new 12-personnel packages
Bridge center with Daboll familiarity steps in after Lloyd Cushenberry release, expected to compete for starting role
Veteran backup reunites with OC Daboll to mentor Cam Ward, likely signaling end of Will Levis era in Tennessee
Two-time Super Bowl champion provides CB depth and special teams value in Tennessee's revamped secondary
Depth interior DL rotates behind Simmons and Franklin-Myers to round out an upgraded defensive front
Former starting guard returning from shoulder surgery adds interior OL competition on a prove-it deal
Experienced punter upgrades Titans special teams unit on a two-year deal

key_losses.contract_summary: Traded (2 yrs remaining on rookie deal)
key_losses.headline: PFF's 5th-ranked DT and elite run-stopper dealt to Jets in player-for-player swap for EDGE Jermaine Johnson — scheme-driven departure under Saleh's 4-3
Failed 4yr/$76.4M Carthon-era signing played just 12 games across two injury-plagued seasons and is expected to be released
Disappointing $23M/yr Carthon signing (4 TDs in 2 seasons) superseded by Robinson, expected to be released or restructured before March 16
Starting center released in late February, creating the IOL hole Tennessee is addressing with bridge options Schlottman and Volson
Rotational pass rusher signs with division rival Colts, further thinning an EDGE room that is now dependent on Johnson's Achilles recovery
Former Titan (traded mid-season) officially departs, inking a $39M deal in New England — adds to pre-FA EDGE depth losses Tennessee had to address
Depth/rotational safety released in late February, thinning the safety room behind Hooker and Diggs

notes: Robinson contract: $70M/4yr with $39.98M fully guaranteed per SI's Albert Breer (most precisely sourced). Some reports cite $78M total — this discrepancy may reflect incentives.

Bellinger contract: Conflicting reports — $24M/3yr (PFF, Arrowhead Pride) vs. $42M/3yr (Music City Miracles). Guarantee details not yet confirmed. Using $42M in headline; flagged as medium confidence.
Trubisky and Schlottman: 2-year and 1-year deals respectively confirmed, but financial terms not yet publicly reported.
Morgan Cox re-signing: 1-year deal, financial terms not reported.
L'Jarius Sneed release: Not yet officially processed as of March 11 (timing tied to injury guarantee triggers on March 16). Treated as a departure given near-certainty of release.
Calvin Ridley release: Not yet official as of March 11. Same March 16 trigger deadline. Treated as a pending departure.
All deals agreed during the legal tampering period (March 9-10) become official March 11 when the new league year opens.
